claim: `P_theta(L|x)` is a well-defined CRF posterior event probability for finite `Y,T` and regular language `L`
boundary: supports the audit object; not a standalone novelty claim

claim: Product automaton transfer computes `Z_{theta,L}(x)` exactly under explicit finite-context/local-factor assumptions
evidence: product transfer tests match brute force for accept-all, empty, exact-pattern, parity, low-probability events
boundary: product-automaton marginal inference is the known computational neighborhood; reference implementation is finite/sanity scale

claim: Hard-constrained decoding and posterior consistency are different objects
evidence: R5a: constrained legality can be 1 while B0 `P(BIO|x)=0.0566`; B1 does not alter posterior mass; B7 reports legal decoding without using event mass
boundary: do not say hard constraints are useless

claim: Event loss has a finite expectation-difference gradient when `Z_{theta,L}(x)>0`
boundary: training-signal statement only; no task-improvement theorem

claim: Semi-event training can raise posterior event mass
evidence: R5a, R1, R2, R4, public CoNLL2000 formal, and R7 sensitivity all show positive B4/event-loss event-mass movement
boundary: secondary empirical evidence; not always task-metric dominant; not benchmark superiority

claim: WNUT17 exposes hidden posterior BIO conflict under diagnostic stress
evidence: R5a B0 hidden conflict near 1 with entity F1 0; B4 raises BIO event mass
boundary: diagnostic stress only; no NER usefulness claim

claim: WNUT17 is learnable by the local CRF setup
evidence: R5b B0 entity F1 `0.1660` over 10 seeds
boundary: modest viability check only; no B4 NER improvement claim; not a main paper claim

claim: In the evaluated field-style diagnostics, low `P_theta(L|x)` has positive ranking signal for exact error
evidence: R6a bottom/top gaps plus reanalysis: AUROC `0.7088`, AUPRC `0.8470`; uncertainty baselines are stronger and complementarity is not robust
boundary: evaluated field-style tasks only; not calibration, not all structured prediction, not uncertainty-baseline superiority, not robust residual predictiveness

claim: Hidden conflict can concentrate at low event mass in semi-real diagnostics, while real-source hidden conflict is often saturated or near zero
evidence: R6a hidden-conflict gaps strongest on amount/date/product_code; R5a supports WNUT hidden conflict
boundary: real-source fields mostly saturated; do not generalize hidden-conflict concentration beyond evaluated diagnostics

claim: Reference product-transfer scaling can be discussed via product-state count
evidence: R8 varies sequence length, label count, DFA states, context order
boundary: reference CPU sanity only; no speed superiority

claim: Conditional nonnegative MPO/rank membership can be stated under explicit assumptions
Positive-cone approximation bounds can be stated under nonnegativity and positivity assumptions
boundary: posterior/log versions need denominator and strict positivity control

claim: The public CoNLL2000 BIO/chunking case provides a structured-prediction audit case with event-mass movement, hidden-conflict accounting, legal constrained decoding, and task-metric boundary behavior
evidence: local full 3-seed run `002ecbb`: B0 `P(BIO|x)=0.9837 +/- 0.0011`, hidden conflict `0.0133`, span F1 `0.7973`; B4 `P(BIO|x)=0.9885 +/- 0.0063`, hidden conflict `0.0123`, span F1 `0.7918`; B7 legality `1.0000`
boundary: public case-study evidence only; B4 raises event mass on average but token/span metrics decrease on average; not SOTA, benchmark superiority, or proof that event training generally improves task metrics

claim: A faithful constrained-product decoding baseline can be reported separately from event-mass auditing
evidence: B7 formal: legal rate `1.0000` for B0 and B4 source models; `uses_event_mass_for_decoding=False`
boundary: constrained-product Viterbi baseline only; not a full WFST toolkit and not a replacement claim

claim: Lambda/rule sensitivity changes event-mass movement and exposes boundary cases where event loss is unhelpful or harmful for task metrics
evidence: R7 wrapped formal derisk run `experiments/runs/local_checks/r7_sensitivity_derisk_formal_wrapped`, config `experiments/configs/exp7/r7_sensitivity_formal.yaml`, returncode `0`, duration `28.276712`: stock-like digits has `legal_rate_not_useful=True`; `product_code_swapped_rule` B4 lambda 1.0 raises `P(event)` by `+0.9485` while char accuracy drops `-0.5524` and exact accuracy drops `-0.0816`
boundary: sensitivity/boundary study only; rule choice matters; event loss is not a general accuracy method or benchmark claim
